title: what next
wikitext:
[]: what next?             Quote Post by Kyle » Thu Jan 19, 2006 8:29 pm we all know that at the end of EOE that Asuka and Shinji are on the beach.Now this may be a stupid question but what did they do after that? Iv heard the whole "there the new adam and eve"bit and they have to restart the human race but i dont think that would happen right away.Did they look for survivers,talk things out,look for food/shelter?what? If Shinji and i have something in comon its we dwell on shit.i have not seen EOE in over a year so it would be nice if i could get some sensible closure.

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Thu Jan 19, 2006 9:08 pm When the creators named the movie "End of Evangelion", that's exactly what they meant: the end. As for interpretation as to what happens next, that is really up to each person's own perception. The "Adam and Eve" theory you mentioned is just that. A theory. Sure, some people believe it, but there are plenty others that do not(myself included). Although if you are intrested in seeing a light-hearted interpretation, may I recommend Evacomics .

[]:             Quote Post by Archon Divinus » Thu Jan 19, 2006 9:25 pm I've always thought that, although they were the last humans, they wouldn't restart the human race. They would live on the remains of earth for a while, and then eventually die.

[]:             Quote Post by AcesHigh » Thu Jan 19, 2006 10:06 pm Asuka breaking a bottle on Shinji -- very accurate / hilarious. Haha.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:24 am Yui's dialog indicates that anyone who can imagine themselves in their own heart again can return to life. The fact that Shinji and Asuka, the two characters least comfortable with their personal identity, would be the first to return is encouraging to me. One would reasonably be led to believe that they are simply the first, and that more people will return to live on. I'm interested to know exactly what state the rest of the world is in. Wherever Shinji and Asuka wind up is pretty damn desolate, and I sure hope for their sake that the rest of the world doesn't look like that.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:33 am Well lets hope that the few people we saw get hit by the meltdown squad aren't the only other returnees, that would suck. Imagine: Hyuga's bad jokes, Aoba's pessimism, Fuyusuki's boring biology lectures, Shinji's whinyness, Asuka's bitchiness, Maya wouldn't be so bad I guess, and Keel's constant bitching. Yeeha sign me up for that group of folks. They would make you wish you hadn't returned. Hopefully Pen-Pen would return. I don't know if all people would know that they could return, I mean Yui told Shinji but what about everyone else? And what about organisms that don't even have a nervous system like bacteria? And really dumb animals like chickens? How could we survive without fried chicken?

[]:             Quote Post by Archon Divinus » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:39 am I kind of have a theory that, of the characters we see, the only other one who could come back would be Aoba. He's the only one who doesn't go into complementation happily. Everyone else is in ecstasy over being turned to goo, but he goes screaming, which is his rejection of complementation.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 2:05 am I think the others would return as soon as they realized what a let-down complementation was. Maya especially she was happy to see Ritsuko, but with her reactions to everything going on I think she would reject it in the end. Fuyusuki would return once he realized he wasn't actually reunited with Yui. I don't know about Makoto, but I think he would return especially if Misato was not involved in completion, but I think Keel would return once he realized that SEELE's plan had partially failed, and other people were returning Besides a guy that strong willed would have a hard time just being in a state that stagnent.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Jan 20, 2006 2:19 am Keel would return once he realized that SEELE's plan had partially failed

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 2:45 am Quote: Keel would return once he realized that SEELE's plan had partially failed I dunno about that, the smart thing to do would be to lay low for awhile, once everyone returns their going to be looking for whose responsible for the mess and he'll get linched if someone in the know like Kaji tells everyone that it was him. So the smart thing to do would be to lay low for awhile until the heat blows over and then return much later.

[]:             Quote Post by Archon Divinus » Fri Jan 20, 2006 6:20 am Theres no way Keel would come back, and if he did, he would probably die imediatly after, because he wouldn't have any of his cyborg implants anymore. Its hard to live with no spine.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 7:14 am .....Than by that logic Shinji and Asuka wouldn't have returned with their clothes on, People would reform with whatever implants they had or with whatever clothes they had on, otherwise everyone with a pacemaker, artficial heart ect... wouldn't live either. People would presumebly just reform out of the same puddles they melted into.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jan 20, 2006 10:13 am Maybe this is all symbolic. As it is in the Bible. Adam and Eve/Shinji and Asuka were expelled from the Garden of Eden/primordial soup (or Nature's womb if you like) but this time around it was of their own freewill? If you took the Bible literally and asked how did Adam and Eve survive outside the Garden you would come across similar problems. Thinking aloud here.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 11:47 am Isn't there a post that says Shinji and Asuka=Adam and Eve no touchy? Yui says the others can come back, Asuka and Shinji weren't expelled from anything, they chose to come back. The sea of LCL wasn't paradise Shinji said it himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:05 pm Heidroch wrote: When the creators named the movie "End of Evangelion", that's exactly what they meant: the end.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:13 pm I though that was what Shinji ought be most concerned about...

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Fri Jan 20, 2006 1:14 pm post-third-impact earth would be a very interesting place to live in, since you basically know everyone else on the planet through-and-through, it'd make the dating scene more interesting at least. ;

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 20, 2006 4:31 pm If you knew everyone that well I doubt you'd want to date them, let alone reproduce with them. I predict the very fast extiction of mankind due to this problem alone after 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Sun Jan 22, 2006 8:19 pm I dont, the basic instinct for sex would over come the warnings of the mind eventually.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jan 22, 2006 8:54 pm TheBlueTree wrote: I dont, the basic instinct for sex would over come the warnings of the mind eventually.
